basics.name: David Barreto Ferreira
basics.label: Backend Engineer & Tech Lead
basics.summary: Backend engineer with 10+ years building distributed systems, mostly in Java, mostly in places where downtime isn't an option. I've shipped trading infrastructure, financial middleware, and offshore tooling — and led the teams doing it. Currently tech lead at Devexperts on the thinkorswim platform. Outside of work I'm exploring Rust, building a NES emulator, and assembling a self-hosted AI assistant I call Alfred.

work.name: Devexperts
work.summary: Leading a backend engineering team responsible for core components of the thinkorswim trading platform, one of the most widely used platforms in retail trading.
highlights: Leading a team of 4–6 engineers across distributed international locations
Designing and maintaining mission-critical trading infrastructure with high availability requirements
Backend development in Java 17 with Oracle DB and distributed messaging systems
Driving architecture decisions, code quality standards and engineering processes
Contributing to production support and incident response for financial-grade systems

work.name: Revolut
work.summary: Backend engineering at one of Europe's largest fintech companies.
highlights: Developed and maintained backend services in a high-scale microservices environment
Worked on financial products serving millions of users across Europe

work.name: Wellhub
work.summary: Backend development for a global corporate wellness platform.
highlights: Built and maintained backend services supporting a global network of gyms and wellness partners
Worked in a distributed team on high-availability microservices

work.name: ALTER SOLUTIONS PORTUGAL
work.summary: Software consultant embedded at BNP Paribas / SLIB developing risk management web applications and services.
highlights: Developed web applications and backend services for risk management systems at BNP Paribas/SLIB
Worked within a regulated financial environment with strict compliance and quality requirements

work.name: B3 – Brasil, Bolsa, Balcão
work.summary: Backend development for the Brazilian stock exchange, focusing on OTC (over-the-counter) systems and market infrastructure.
highlights: Designed and implemented middleware and system integrations for the OTC department
Worked on NoMe (Novo Mercado), the insurance registration system and the Cetip21 internal framework
Automated software infrastructure maintenance across critical financial systems
Operated in a highly regulated, high-reliability environment processing billions in daily transactions

work.name: Prill Tecnologia
work.summary: Technical leadership for software projects in the oil & gas and financial sectors.
highlights: Led technical proposals, resource planning and architecture design for client projects
Managed project delivery, requirements analysis and R&D initiatives
Modelled software architecture for distributed enterprise systems
Bridged technical and business stakeholders across complex multi-team projects

work.name: Prill Tecnologia
work.summary: Full-stack backend development for enterprise clients in oil & gas, offshore and financial sectors.
highlights: Built enterprise applications using Java EE, EJB, JMS, JPA, Hibernate and Apache Camel
Developed integrations using SOAP/REST web services, XML/XPath/XSLT and Apache CXF
Worked with Oracle Database, PL/SQL, MySQL and application servers including WildFly and Oracle WebLogic
Used Oracle Coherence for distributed caching in high-availability deployments
Contributed to offshore and oil & gas techno-scientific applications using C/C++ and Borland C++ Builder

work.name: Mathematics and Statistics Institute – Rio de Janeiro State University
work.summary: Taught undergraduate programming courses at UERJ.
highlights: Taught Object-Oriented Programming with Java for Computer Science undergraduates
Taught Introduction to Computing and Programming for Engineering students

work.name: Tempo Laboratory – Fluminense Federal University
work.summary: Research in real-time, distributed and ubiquitous computing systems at UFF's Tempo Laboratory.
highlights: Researched distributed systems, sensor networks, mobile and ubiquitous computing
Developed middleware and applications for smart spaces and context-aware environments
Work formed the basis of MSc thesis on pervasive computing (SmartAndroid platform)

work.name: Quetzal Development of Digital Systems
work.summary: Software development internship focused on techno-scientific applications for offshore companies.
highlights: Developed and maintained applications for offshore and oil & gas clients
Worked primarily in C/C++ and Borland C++ Builder

education.area: Networks and Parallel and Distributed Systems
education.summary: Research in ubiquitous and pervasive computing. Developed the SmartAndroid middleware platform for context-aware smart environments.

awards.title: Honorable Mention
awards.awarder: ACM ICPC – International Collegiate Programming Contest
awards.summary: Honorable mention in the ICPC, the most prestigious competitive programming contest for university students.

projects.name: NES Emulator
projects.description: Nintendo Entertainment System emulator written in Rust with a custom MOS 6502 CPU implementation.

projects.name: Alfred
projects.description: Self-hosted personal AI assistant platform integrating n8n automation, a Python backend, Claude and local LLMs, Telegram, Google Calendar and Gmail.

projects.name: SmartAndroid
projects.description: Research middleware platform for ubiquitous and context-aware smart environments. Developed during MSc at UFF.
